The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This is a sample plain-text
file used to test Shannon entropy scoring. Ordinary English prose has a
fairly predictable letter distribution, so its entropy per byte sits
noticeably below random or compressed data -- typically in the 4 to 5
bits-per-byte range, since common letters like 'e' and spaces recur far
more often than rare characters like 'q' or 'z'. Repetition and predictable
structure are exactly what keep entropy low, which is why plain text is
easy to compress and easy to distinguish from packed or encrypted payloads.
